sections.content:

It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.

- Object taxonomy: {'divider_blocks': 'White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, can be swapped with objects via UP/DOWN', 'colored_pixels': 'Small 2x2 movable objects inside containers on left panel', 'gray_containers': '4x8 gray boxes on left that hold colored pixels', 'white_containers': 'White frames on right panel containing colored shapes (red and blue)', 'blue_square': '2x4 blue block on left panel', 'red_blocks': 'Red and dark red blocks on left panel', 'pixel_pattern': 'Scattered blue/charcoal 1px objects forming a pattern on left'}
- HUD map: {'bottom_bar': 'White horizontal bar at row 63, shrinks as timer/progress indicator', 'divider': 'Vertical line of white 2x2 blocks at col 32-33, rows 10-50'}
Current level:
- World model: Two-panel layout: left (charcoal bg) has colored objects in gray containers; right (black bg) has colored objects in white containers. Vertical white divider at col 32-33. Arrow keys move active object: LEFT/RIGHT within container, UP/DOWN swaps with divider blocks. Green pixel (node 34) is currently the active object, restored to original position after DOWN.
- Goal model: Move colored objects from left containers through divider to right side to match targets in white containers. Need to figure out how to select different objects.
- Recent findings: ['UP swaps active object with divider block above', 'DOWN reverses the swap', 'LEFT/RIGHT move within container bounds', 'Node IDs track objects across swaps - colors move with nodes']
- Open questions: ["How to select/activate different objects? (MOUSE doesn't seem to change selection)", 'What is the target configuration on the right side?', 'Does UP always go to the nearest divider block, or a specific one?', 'Can objects be moved from right side back to left?']
- Plan: Try clicking on different objects to see if selection changes, then use arrow keys to move them. Need to move all left-side objects to right-side containers.
- Failed probes: ['MOUSE(20,24) on yellow pixel: only advanced timer, no gameplay change (step 1)']

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! After LEFT, LEFT, UP:
1. LEFT: Green moved from [[38,12],[39,13]] to [[38,10],[39,11]]
2. LEFT: Green moved from [[38,10],[39,11]] to [[38,8],[39,9]]
3. UP: Nothing happened! Green stayed at [[38,8],[39,9]] and is still green (id=32).
Wait, but earlier when green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and I pressed UP, it DID swap with the divider. Now at [[38,8],[39,9]], UP did nothing. Let me check:
- Green is at [[38,8],[39,9]] - this is the LEFT edge of the narrow part
- Earlier swap happened when green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] - this was the RIGHT edge of the narrow part
So maybe the swap only happens at the RIGHT edge of the narrow part (cols 10-11), not the LEFT edge (cols 8-9)?
Let me try RIGHT to move green back to cols 10-11, then UP again.
Also, I notice the ascii shows:
Row 38: ccccWWBBBBBBBW
Row 39: ccccWWBBBBBBBW
The 'WW' at positions 4-5 in the substring (cols 32-33 in grid) are the divider blocks. They're still white. The green pixel didn't swap with them.
Let me try RIGHT then UP.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# UP from [[38,8],[39,9]] did nothing - green stayed
# Earlier swap happened at [[40,10],[41,11]] (right edge of narrow part)
# Hypothesis: swap only happens at cols 10-11, not cols 8-9

# Move RIGHT to cols 10-11, then try UP
action(['RIGHT'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Now try UP from cols 10-11
action(['UP'])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print(f"\nAfter UP from cols 10-11:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        b = n['boundary']
        if 36 <= b[0][0] <= 44:
            print(f"White near divider: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check if anything changed
print(f"board_changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed')}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "Moves active object right 2px within container (VERIFIED)", "LEFT": "Moves active object left 2px within container (VERIFIED)", "UP": "At cols 10-11 rows 40-41: swaps with divider. At cols 8-9: no effect. At cols 12-13: moves up within container (VERIFIED)", "DOWN": "At cols 10-11: swaps with divider. At cols 12-13: moves down within container (VERIFIED)", "MOUSE": "Does NOT change active object (VERIFIED)"}, "object_taxonomy": {"green_pixel": "Active player object, 2x2", "divider_blocks": "White 2x2 blocks at col 32-33"}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "White bar at row 63"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Green at [[38,8],[39,9]]. UP only swaps with divider at specific positions (cols 10-11, rows 40-41 worked before).", "goal_model": "Move green pixel through divider to right side", "recent_findings": ["UP at cols 8-9 does nothing", "UP at cols 10-11 rows 40-41 swapped with divider earlier"], "open_questions": ["Does UP at cols 10-11 rows 38-39 also swap? What row matters?"], "current_plan": "Move RIGHT to cols 10-11, try UP", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["UP at cols 8-9 - no change"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

After UP from cols 10-11:
Green: id=33 boundary=[[38, 10], [38, 11], [39, 11], [39, 10]]
White near divider: id=34 boundary=[[38, 32], [38, 33], [39, 33], [39, 32]]
